audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Joshua / Yehoshua southern coalition, sun-and-moon poem, five-kings execution, and city-destruction campaign, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Gibeonite treaty defense, five-king coalition, hailstones, Book of Jashar poem, ancient celestial language, cave imprisonment, foot-on-neck humiliation, sunset burial, and repeated total-destruction formulas.

verses.restored: It was, when Adoni-zedek king of Jerusalem heard that Joshua captured Ai and devoted it to destruction—as he did to Jericho and its king, so he did to Ai and its king—and that inhabitants of Gibeon made peace with Israel and were in their midst,
that they feared greatly, because Gibeon was a great city, like one of royal cities, and because it was greater than Ai and all its men were mighty.
Adoni-zedek king of Jerusalem sent to Hoham king of Hebron, Piram king of Jarmuth, Japhia king of Lachish, and Debir king of Eglon, saying,
Come up to me and help me, and let us strike Gibeon, because it made peace with Joshua and children of Israel.

Five kings of Amorite gathered and went up: king of Jerusalem, king of Hebron, king of Jarmuth, king of Lachish, and king of Eglon, they and all their camps; they camped against Gibeon and fought against it.

Men of Gibeon sent to Joshua at camp at Gilgal, saying, Do not slacken your hands from your servants. Come up to us quickly, save us and help us, for all kings of Amorite dwelling in hill country have gathered against us.
Joshua went up from Gilgal, he and all people of war with him, and all mighty men of strength.
YHWH said to Joshua, Do not fear them, for into your hand I have given them; not a man from them shall stand before you.
Joshua came upon them suddenly; all night he went up from Gilgal.
YHWH confounded them before Israel, and he struck them with a great striking at Gibeon, pursued them by way of ascent of Beth-horon, and struck them as far as Azekah and Makkedah.

It was, as they fled from before Israel on descent of Beth-horon, that YHWH cast upon them great stones from heavens as far as Azekah, and they died; more died from hailstones than children of Israel killed with sword.
Then Joshua spoke to YHWH on day YHWH gave Amorite before children of Israel, and he said before eyes of Israel: Sun, be still at Gibeon, and moon, in valley of Aijalon.
Sun stood still and moon stayed until nation took vengeance upon its enemies. Is it not written in Scroll of Jashar? Sun stood in midst of heavens and did not hasten to go down for about a whole day.
There was no day like it before it or after it, that YHWH listened to voice of a man, for YHWH fought for Israel.
Joshua returned, and all Israel with him, to camp at Gilgal.
Those five kings fled and hid themselves in cave at Makkedah.
It was told to Joshua, saying, The five kings have been found hidden in cave at Makkedah.
Joshua said, Roll great stones to mouth of cave and appoint men over it to guard them.
But you, do not stand; pursue after your enemies and strike their rear. Do not let them enter their cities, for YHWH your Divine has given them into your hand.
It was, when Joshua and children of Israel finished striking them with a very great striking until they came to an end, and survivors who survived from them entered fortified cities,
that all people returned to Joshua at camp at Makkedah in peace; no one sharpened/moved his tongue against any man of children of Israel.
Joshua said, Open mouth of cave and bring out to me those five kings from cave.
They did so and brought out to him from cave those five kings: king of Jerusalem, king of Hebron, king of Jarmuth, king of Lachish, and king of Eglon.

It was, when they brought out those kings to Joshua, that Joshua called all men of Israel and said to commanders of men of war who went with him, Draw near; put your feet upon necks of these kings. They drew near and put their feet upon their necks.
Joshua said to them, Do not fear and do not be shattered; be strong and courageous, for thus YHWH will do to all your enemies against whom you fight.
Afterward Joshua struck them and put them to death and hanged them upon five trees, and they were hanging upon trees until evening.

It was at time of going down of sun that Joshua commanded, and they took them down from trees and threw them into cave where they had hidden and placed great stones at mouth of cave, to this very day.

Joshua captured Makkedah on that day and struck it by edge of sword; its king he devoted to destruction, them and every soul/life in it. He left no survivor and did to king of Makkedah as he did to king of Jericho.
Joshua passed from Makkedah, and all Israel with him, to Libnah and fought against Libnah.
YHWH also gave it and its king into hand of Israel, and he struck it by edge of sword and every soul/life in it. He left no survivor in it and did to its king as he did to king of Jericho.
Joshua passed from Libnah, and all Israel with him, to Lachish; he camped against it and fought against it.
YHWH gave Lachish into hand of Israel, and he captured it on second day and struck it by edge of sword and every soul/life in it, according to all he did to Libnah.
Then Horam king of Gezer went up to help Lachish, and Joshua struck him and his people until he left him no survivor.
Joshua passed from Lachish, and all Israel with him, to Eglon; they camped against it and fought against it.
They captured it on that day and struck it by edge of sword, and every soul/life in it on that day he devoted to destruction, according to all he did to Lachish.
Joshua went up from Eglon, and all Israel with him, to Hebron, and they fought against it.

They captured it and struck it by edge of sword, its king, all its cities, and every soul/life in it. He left no survivor, according to all he did to Eglon; he devoted it to destruction and every soul/life in it.
Joshua turned, and all Israel with him, to Debir and fought against it.

He captured it, its king, and all its cities; they struck them by edge of sword and devoted every soul/life in it to destruction. He left no survivor. As he did to Hebron, so he did to Debir and its king, and as he did to Libnah and its king.
Joshua struck all land: hill country, Negev, Shephelah, and slopes, and all their kings. He left no survivor and devoted every breath to destruction, as YHWH Divine of Israel commanded.
Joshua struck them from Kadesh-barnea as far as Gaza and all land of Goshen as far as Gibeon.
Joshua captured all these kings and their land in one campaign, because YHWH Divine of Israel fought for Israel.
Joshua returned, and all Israel with him, to camp at Gilgal.

translation_notes: Israel's treaty with Gibeon creates an obligation to defend the threatened Gibeonites, showing that the oath has practical force despite its deceptive origin.

The sun-and-moon passage is poetic ancient celestial language cited from the Scroll of Jashar, a lost source also named elsewhere. It should not be forced into modern astronomy or used to attack science.

Whether the poem describes prolonged daylight, cessation of celestial movement, silence, or another ancient image remains debated; the source-audited layer preserves its wording without pseudoscientific certainty.
Hailstones and sword are both agents of mass death. Miracle language does not make the violence ethically normative.
Placing feet on defeated kings' necks is ritualized domination and humiliation, not a model for leadership or treatment of captives.
The kings are executed, displayed, and removed by sunset in continuity with Deuteronomy's corpse law.

Verses 28-40 repeat no-survivor and every-breath destruction formulas across civilian cities. These are explicit genocide texts and may never be sanitized, reenacted, or transferred to modern populations.
The claimed single-campaign scope and historical geography require literary, archaeological, and textual audit rather than triumphalist certainty.
safety_note: Never use Joshua 10 to justify genocide, celestial pseudoscience, holy war, humiliation of prisoners, execution of captives, collective punishment, or modern territorial conquest. Preserve the poem and narrative honestly while keeping every destroyed city and civilian life visible under categorical moral protest.
